question_text: A heating element has a resistance of 20Ω and draws 5A of current. Calculate the power dissipated by the heating element and explain why this makes it useful for heating applications.
explanation: Using P = I²R = (5A)² × 20Ω = 25 × 20 = 500W, or P = IV where V = IR = 5A × 20Ω = 100V, so P = 5A × 100V = 500W. High power dissipation means electrical energy is efficiently converted to heat, making it ideal for heating.
